hero.tagline: I design systems and infrastructure; I automate and ship software that works.

about.title: About me
about.bio_1: I'm Raúl Rivero Matesanz, a Software Engineer from UPM, with an Erasmus stay at Hanze University (Groningen, Feb–Jun 2022). I've been in DevOps since 2021. At Lacía I focus on platform support and operations on Azure—pipelines, deployments, and keeping infra dependable for developers.
about.bio_2: I live in Madrid, in Ensanche de Vallecas, on my own since May 2024. I share my home with Kali, a dog adopted in August 2024. I'm passionate about automation, CI/CD, and cloud architecture; I like leaving systems clear and maintainable.
about.interests_value: Live music · Fantasy literature · Calisthenics · Volunteering
about.soft_skill_1: Clear communication with technical and business teams: turning problems into concrete actions.
about.soft_skill_2: Teamwork and collaboration in agile settings, prioritising shared outcomes.
about.soft_skill_3: Adaptability when priorities, incidents, or tools change.
about.soft_skill_4: Systems thinking: weighing impact on pipelines, operations, and users before deciding.

value_delivery.title: Shipping value early
value_delivery.body: I favour small, frequent releases that unblock others: less waiting, real feedback sooner, and less batch risk.

observability.title: Observability
observability.body: Without visibility there is no sustainable improvement: metrics, logs, and traces used well cut diagnosis time and reduce blind firefighting.

iteration.title: Iteration and continuous improvement
iteration.body: Each cycle should leave the system slightly better: automate repetition, document gaps, and refine process with what production teaches.

testing.title: Testing
testing.body: Tests are not optional extras—they are the safety net that lets you change with confidence, from pipelines to checks that protect what matters.

documentation.title: Documentation
documentation.body: What is not written is forgotten or trapped in one person. Short, up-to-date, discoverable docs are part of the product, not an afterthought.

judgment.title: Judgment and tooling
judgment.body: I evaluate new tools with the same rigor as a production change: cost, time, vendor lock-in, and privacy. I don't adopt trends for their own sake; what matters is maintainability and clear boundaries for sensitive data.
